item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The forced sarkic connection really kicked this good idea until it stopped moving and then set the remains on fire. I'll have to downvote, even if the rest of the article is really good, because I feel that choice on your part was really misguided and you could have made the article better doing (almost literally) anything else. Could you please consider removing it? It's a shame, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was 100% off-board when I saw the first two "non-human entity with an entirely different way of experiencing the world expresses itself in generic hostility" interviews. The third one got me interested again, to the point that I was ready to even upvote. Then the fourth interview brought it back down to novote territory.

I feel like "the skin is a liar" is a much better place to end the story than "the skin is trying to re-ascend to godhood as part of a spooky ritual of the Xkthkoffeeklatchj." Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
TYPO!

(Where is says SCP-2963-6 was silent for 30 seconds, it says SCP-2963-2. I had to put it out!)

EDIT: Fixed it myself. I am sorry if I was no allowed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would you say that the twist came out of nowhere and was all bone but no meat?… I'm sorry for that pun
Edit: Disregard what I said after this, as I don't think I'm supposed to post comments of that vein.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Show opinion post
- Hide click HERE to close

This one’s pretty alright. It’s evident that they treat each individual section as a different individual, which is pretty interesting.

-1 is treated with respect because it gives respect (and is most obviously the original person, who wasn’t exactly a murderer and was given professional respect by researchers), -3 has no real ability to self-control and the Doctor is stricter with hierarchy, -2 was spoken with politely and straightforward because it communicates so rarely.

Professional but succinct, the doctor stays only as long as necessary with each interview.

Not a big fan of the Vinz Clortho cameo but I’m reasonably certain that most Sarkic type SCPs are intentionally OTT in that regard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could also have done without the implied Sarkic connection, and I'm not sure why Dr. McGann suddenly decides to use its name in the -6 interview when they were against it in the -3 interview, but the article still charmed me enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was wavering between a neutral vote and a downvote at the point of the interview with the muscles; the interview was kind of reminiscent of the typical angry D-Class what doesn't like being subject to crimes against humanity, but this thing is so goddamn weird that my interest was held. Then the skeleton interview came, and I was simultaneously glad to see it free of internet memes and also pointing to something deeper going on here. Then the skin interview hit, and I was off the proverbial hook.

I could see this working with some changes to it, and I'm willing to roll with your initial premise, but I found the conclusion kind of a let down, given the buildup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They do something similar for burn victims- skin is useful for a number of reasons, keeping your insides moist is one of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This sems way too similiar to [[SCP-086]], and doesn't do anything interesting. The "pulling people from other dimensions" thing was kind of boring and out of nowhere.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot. My only issue, which is more of a nitpick, is the specification for the humidity level of the containment area. I didn't see any reason in the containment procedures or the description to merit its inclusion. Unless I completely missed it, in which case feel free to point it out.

You get a +1 from me regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't actually reference Sarkicism anywhere (at least not that I can find). Could be that these are just unaffiliated beings from beyond, one of which happens to ramble about it's apotheosis (a common goal for lesser eldritch entities, from what I'm given to understand)

Edit: Oh, there. I stand by my original statement while adding "ha-ha alternate universe sarkicist got screwed over." XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why'd it have to be Sarkicism? Why couldn't the beings from beyond exist on their own? Why does everyone feel like their body horror SCP isn't complete unless it references some Karcist or Klavigar or Koka-kola with several K's in the name?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh. Novote.

I liked the idea of a D-class getting broken down by their various body systems, and each of those systems becoming its own entity. I even liked that you gave each system a differing version of the originals personality. However, we eventually get to the twist with the skin, and the skeleton, and it just fell flat for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: a talking skeleton AND a really nice concept, definite +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, a subtle sarkic post. I like it. My only gripe is that the reference seems to be out of the blue, with little to no reference in previous interviews.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the concept and how it's presented.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Decided to dust off an old draft I had lying around and refurbish it for the contest. Hope you like it.
